factors.summary: Geopolitical factors such as trade tensions and sanctions pose risks to the company's operations and markets.

evidence.text: • economic, political and business conditions in the markets in which we operate, including changes to trade agreements, sanctions, import and export regulations and custom duties; • conditions of the institutional, commercial and residential construction and remodeling markets, including the impact of work-from-home trends; • fluctuations in currency exchange rates; • availability of and fluctuations in the prices of key commodities and the impact of higher energy prices; • potential further impairment of our goodwill, indefinite-lived intangible assets and/or our long-lived assets; • interest rate fluctuations and other changes in borrowing costs, in addition to risks associated with our outstanding and future indebtedness; • the impact our outstanding indebtedness may have on our business and operations and other capital market conditions, including availability of funding sources and currency exchange rate fluctuations; • risks related to corporate social responsibility and reputational matters; • the ability to protect our brand reputation and trademarks; • the outcome of any litigation, governmental investigations or proceedings; • claims of infringement of intellectual property rights by third parties; • adverse publicity or improper conduct by any of our employees, agents or business partners; • changes to, or changes in interpretations of, current laws and regulations; • uncertainty and inherent subjectivity related to transfer pricing regulations; • changes in tax requirements, including tax rate changes, the adoption of new tax legislation or exposure to additional tax liabilities and revised tax law interpretations; and • risks related to our incorporation in Ireland, including the possible effects on us of future legislation or interpretations in the U.S. that may limit or eliminate potential U.S. tax benefits resulting from our incorporation in a non-U.S. jurisdiction, such as Ireland, or deny U.S. government contracts to us based upon our incorporation in such non-U.S. jurisdiction.

factors.summary: The company is demonstrating strong pricing power, successfully raising prices in response to both lower volumes and rising costs. While this has partially offset impacts on net revenues, it reflects a strategic approach to managing cost increases.

evidence.text: The decrease in Net revenues was partially offset by improved pricing and the impact of foreign currency exchange rate movements.
We are implementing pricing initiatives to address rising production, material and freight costs.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with strong demand in most markets but constrained by supply chain disruptions and material shortages.

evidence.text: The decrease in Net revenues was driven by lower volumes resulting from the supply chain disruptions and delays and shortages of materials, components and labor discussed above.
During the third quarter of 2021, we continued to see strong and accelerating demand for our products and services in most of the markets we serve.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: The company is experiencing growth in its backlog orders, particularly in non-residential products, driven by robust demand and supply chain challenges. However, these challenges are also causing inefficiencies and production interruptions that negatively impact productivity and margins.

evidence.text: Further, the combination of these factors have also culminated in our highest non-residential product backlog to date as a company.

However, we have also experienced an acceleration of several macroeconomic challenges in the current quarter that have negatively impacted our ability to meet this robust demand [...] These challenges have also created inefficiencies, including periodic production interruptions, that have negatively impacted our productivity and margin performance during the quarter.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products, particularly electronic security products, which are experiencing growth but also facing challenges such as component shortages and supply chain disruptions. This shift is a strategic focus for management and investors, though it has resulted in decreased net revenues in certain segments due to these challenges.

evidence.text: Growth in electronic security products and solutions has become an increased metric monitored by management and a focus of our investors.
Net revenues from the sale of electronic products in the Allegion Americas segment decreased by a high single digits percent compared to the same period in the prior year.

The decrease in Net revenues was principally due to lower volumes in our Allegion Americas segment, resulting from the supply chain disruptions and delays and shortages in materials, components and labor discussed above.
These decreases were partially offset by improved pricing and the impact of foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: Volumes are declining due to supply chain disruptions, material shortages, and lower demand. The decreases were partially offset by improved pricing and foreign currency exchange rate movements.

evidence.text: The decrease in Net revenues was driven by lower volumes resulting from the supply chain disruptions and delays and shortages of materials, components and labor discussed above.

The decrease in Net revenues was principally due to lower volumes in our Allegion Americas segment, resulting from the supply chain disruptions and delays and shortages in materials, components and labor discussed above. Also contributing to the decrease in Net revenues was the divestiture of our QMI business in February 2021. These decreases were partially offset by improved pricing and the impact of foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, particularly in non-residential products, due to supply chain issues.

evidence.text: Net revenues from non-residential products for the three months ended September 30, 2021, decreased by a low single-digits percent compared to the same period in the prior year.

Net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2021, decreased by 1.6%, or $11.4 million, compared with the same period in 2020, due to the following: [...] The decrease in Net revenues was principally due to lower volumes in our Allegion Americas segment, resulting from the supply chain disruptions and delays and shortages in materials, components and labor discussed above. Also contributing to the decrease in Net revenues was the divestiture of our QMI business in February 2021. These decreases were partially offset by improved pricing and the impact of foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising significantly across multiple areas, including commodities, materials, components, packaging, freight, and labor. These increases are driven by inflation and supply chain challenges, which have led to inefficiencies and production interruptions. While the company is adapting to these challenges, the impact on margins and productivity has been negative, particularly affecting the Allegion Americas segment.

evidence.text: Inflation in excess of pricing and productivity reflects the impacts of the increased commodity, material component, packaging, freight and labor inflation, as well as the inefficiencies caused by more acute supply chain challenges and shortages of materials, components and labor discussed above.

These challenges have also created inefficiencies, including periodic production interruptions, that have negatively impacted our productivity and margin performance during the quarter. While these challenges are impacting all our global businesses, to date they have had a more pronounced impact on our Allegion Americas operating segment. [...] We are rapidly adapting to navigate these challenges, and we expect to be well-positioned to convert demand to revenue once conditions normalize.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, leading to margin compression and productivity challenges. The impact is more pronounced in the Allegion Americas segment, contributing to overall margin pressure.

evidence.text: The decreases in Segment operating income and Segment operating margin were primarily driven by inflation and productivity challenges in excess of pricing improvements, unfavorable volume/product mix, increased investment spending and foreign currency exchange rate movements.

These challenges have also created inefficiencies, including periodic production interruptions, that have negatively impacted our productivity and margin performance during the quarter. While these challenges are impacting all our global businesses, to date they have had a more pronounced impact on our Allegion Americas operating segment.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: The end-market momentum for the Allegion Americas segment shows mixed performance. While the residential market is outperforming non-residential markets due to strong DIY projects and housing construction, the segment itself is experiencing slower growth compared to Allegion International, with a 1.6% decrease in net revenues. This decline is attributed to supply chain disruptions, material shortages, labor issues, and the divestiture of the QMI business.

evidence.text: Net revenues from residential products for the three months ended September 30, 2021, decreased by a high single digits percent compared to the same period in the prior year. Nonetheless, we continue to see strong demand for our residential products due to a sustained level of DIY home projects and a robust residential housing construction market.

Net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2021, decreased by 1.6%, or $11.4 million, compared with the same period in 2020, due to the following: [...] The decrease in Net revenues was principally due to lower volumes in our Allegion Americas segment, resulting from the supply chain disruptions and delays and shortages in materials, components and labor discussed above. Also contributing to the decrease in Net revenues was the divestiture of our QMI business in February 2021. These decreases were partially offset by improved pricing and the impact of foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: Margins are compressing due to inflation and productivity challenges outweighing pricing improvements.

evidence.text: The decreases in Segment operating income and Segment operating margin were primarily driven by inflation in excess of pricing and productivity improvements, unfavorable volume/product mix, increased investment spending and foreign currency exchange rate movements.
The decrease in Operating income was primarily due to inflation and productivity challenges in excess of pricing improvements, unfavorable volume/product mix and increased investment spending.
The decrease in Operating margin was primarily due to inflation and productivity challenges in excess of pricing improvements, unfavorable volume/product mix and increased investment spending.

factors.summary: Productivity is negatively impacted by supply chain disruptions and labor shortages, leading to decreased margins and operating income.

evidence.text: The decreases in Segment operating income and Segment operating margin were primarily driven by inflation in excess of pricing and productivity improvements, unfavorable volume/product mix, increased investment spending and foreign currency exchange rate movements.
These challenges have also created inefficiencies, including periodic production interruptions, that have negatively impacted our productivity and margin performance during the quarter.

evidence.text: The decrease in Operating income was primarily due to inflation and productivity challenges in excess of pricing improvements, unfavorable volume/product mix and increased investment spending.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: The company is taking proactive steps to mitigate supply chain issues and reduce costs by re-engineering product designs and seeking alternative suppliers. These actions are aimed at improving operational efficiency and financial performance while minimizing disruptions to customers.

evidence.text: As a result, we are actively implementing measures to mitigate the operational and distribution inefficiencies these component shortages and other challenges are creating, such as re-engineering product designs and configurations and developing alternate sources of supply.

We are rapidly adapting to navigate these challenges, and we expect to be well-positioned to convert demand to revenue once conditions normalize. We remain focused on providing exceptional service to our customers; implementing measures to mitigate operational and distribution inefficiencies and reduce backlogs, such as aligning resources to re-engineer product designs and configurations and developing alternate sources of supply; [...] We will continue to explore various options to control costs and enhance financial performance, while minimizing disruption to customers and the overall business.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is likely constrained due to supply chain disruptions and labor shortages, with no specific data provided.

evidence.text: No specific information on capacity utilization is provided in the text.

However, we have also experienced an acceleration of several macroeconomic challenges in the current quarter that have negatively impacted our ability to meet this robust demand, such as supply chain disruptions and delays; shortages in materials including reductions in allocations of electronic components and other parts from key suppliers; labor shortages; and increased commodity, material component, packaging, freight and labor inflation. These challenges have also created inefficiencies, including periodic production interruptions, that have negatively impacted our productivity and margin performance during the quarter.

factors.summary: The supply chain is facing significant disruptions, primarily due to component shortages and labor shortages, which have led to production interruptions and delays. These issues have also been exacerbated by inflation and logistical challenges, negatively impacting productivity and margins.

evidence.text: This has led to a reduction in the allocations of electronic components we receive from key suppliers, periodic production interruptions and delays in our ability to meet the elevated level of demand for our electronic products during the current quarter.

However, we have also experienced an acceleration of several macroeconomic challenges in the current quarter that have negatively impacted our ability to meet this robust demand, such as supply chain disruptions and delays; shortages in materials including reductions in allocations of electronic components and other parts from key suppliers; labor shortages; and increased commodity, material component, packaging, freight and labor inflation. These challenges have also created inefficiencies, including periodic production interruptions, that have negatively impacted our productivity and margin performance during the quarter.

factors.summary: Management exhibits mixed confidence, showing cautious optimism in addressing challenges while acknowledging uncertainties.

evidence.text: As a result, we are actively implementing measures to mitigate the operational and distribution inefficiencies these component shortages and other challenges are creating, such as re-engineering product designs and configurations and developing alternate sources of supply.

We remain focused on providing exceptional service to our customers; implementing measures to mitigate operational and distribution inefficiencies and reduce backlogs, such as aligning resources to re-engineer product designs and configurations and developing alternate sources of supply; implementing pricing initiatives to address rising production, material and freight costs; and investing in business initiatives to drive future growth. We will continue to explore various options to control costs and enhance financial performance, while minimizing disruption to customers and the overall business; however, the full impacts of the pandemic and the on-going macroeconomic challenges on our business, results of operations, financial condition and cash flows remain uncertain.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: The company faces multiple near-term risks, including ongoing supply chain challenges, component shortages, inflation, labor shortages, and the lingering effects of the COVID-19 pandemic. While some challenges are expected to persist beyond 2021, the company is actively implementing measures to mitigate these issues, such as re-engineering product designs, developing alternate supply sources, and adapting to industry-wide disruptions.

evidence.text: While considered temporary, we expect these challenges around the availability of electronic components to continue beyond 2021. As a result, we are actively implementing measures to mitigate the operational and distribution inefficiencies these component shortages and other challenges are creating, such as re-engineering product designs and configurations and developing alternate sources of supply.

While we currently anticipate these industry-wide challenges to continue beyond 2021, we are rapidly adapting to navigate these challenges, and we expect to be well-positioned to convert demand to revenue once conditions normalize.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: The company is actively returning value to shareholders through a combination of dividend payments and share repurchases, reflecting management's confidence in the business. The increase in cash used for these activities suggests a commitment to maintaining shareholder returns.

evidence.text: Net cash used in financing activities during the nine months ended September 30, 2021, increased $130.0 million compared to the same period in 2020, primarily due to an increase of $118.6 million in cash used to repurchase shares.
During the nine months ended September 30, 2021, we paid dividends of $1.08 per ordinary share to shareholders and repurchased approximately 1.8 million shares for $212.7 million.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, supported by increased net earnings and working capital improvements, while remaining stable and efficiently managed, enabling dividend payments and share repurchases.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities during the nine months ended September 30, 2021, increased $67.0 million compared to the same period in 2020, primarily driven by increased Net earnings and improvements in working capital.
During the nine months ended September 30, 2021, we paid dividends of $1.08 per ordinary share to shareholders and repurchased approximately 1.8 million shares for $212.7 million.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rates on the company is mixed. While there has been a minor positive effect from lower rates, as evidenced by a slight decrease in interest expense, the company also faces risks related to fluctuations in interest rates which could affect borrowing costs and overall financial performance.

evidence.text: Interest expense for the three months ended September 30, 2021, decreased $0.6 million compared with the same period in 2020, primarily due to a lower weighted-average interest rate on our outstanding indebtedness.
• interest rate fluctuations and other changes in borrowing costs, in addition to risks associated with our outstanding and future indebtedness;

factors.summary: The company has shown active merger and acquisition (M&A) activity, including the acquisition of Workforce IT and assets from Astrum Benelux, which contributed to revenue growth. However, the company also faces risks related to potential acquisitions and business opportunities outside its core business, as well as the challenges of integrating acquired assets and managing related risks.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities during the nine months ended September 30, 2021, decreased $24.4 million compared to the same period in 2020, primarily due to cash proceeds from the sales of our equity method investment in Nuki and other investments, as well as a decrease in capital expenditures. Partially offsetting these decreases was an increase due to cash paid for our acquisition of Workforce IT and the assets acquired from Astrum Benelux.
In July 2021, we acquired certain assets of Astrum Benelux B.V. [...] Both WorkforceIT and the assets acquired from Astrum Benelux have been integrated into our Allegion International segment.

These challenges include volatility in global markets, fluctuations in currency exchange rates, higher energy prices, and interest rate fluctuations, all of which create uncertainty and pose risks to the business environment.
